---
title: "Microsoft Copilot Gets a Critique Layer: GPT Drafts, Claude Checks"
date: "2026-03-31 08:00"
author: "AI News"
categories: ["AI Tools & Frameworks", "LLMs & Models"]
tags: ["Microsoft", "Copilot", "Anthropic", "Claude", "GPT", "AI Agents"]
summary: "Microsoft announces a new 'Critique' feature for Microsoft 365 Copilot that uses Anthropic's Claude to review and correct responses generated by OpenAI's GPT inside Copilot Researcher."
---

Microsoft announced on March 30, 2026, a significant update to its Copilot product that marks a notable shift in enterprise AI strategy. The new "Critique" feature within Microsoft 365 Copilot uses Anthropic's Claude to review and correct responses generated by OpenAI's GPT inside Copilot's Researcher agent.

## How It Works

The Critique feature acts as a quality control layer in Copilot's deep research workflows. After GPT generates initial responses, Claude evaluates them for accuracy, completeness, and citation quality before the user ever sees the result. This hybrid approach leverages the strengths of both AI systems—GPT for generation and Claude for refinement.

## Enterprise AI Strategy

This represents a notable departure from the typical AI vendor rivalry. Microsoft is positioning itself not as a model developer but as an orchestrator of best-in-class AI capabilities. By combining OpenAI's generative strengths with Anthropic's批判性 thinking (critical thinking), Microsoft is betting its advantage lies in data integration and workflow optimization rather than model development alone.

## Industry Implications

The move signals a potential trend toward multi-model AI orchestration in enterprise environments. Rather than relying on a single AI provider, companies may increasingly adopt hybrid approaches that pair different models for different tasks. Microsoft's critique feature demonstrates how competing AI companies can coexist within a single product ecosystem when they serve complementary roles.
